validators.name: agent-review
validators.rubric: Review the produced code artifact for exact task fidelity, correctness, and scope control. Pass only if the artifact satisfies the submitted task, keeps edits tightly scoped to owned_paths and expected_files, and preserves existing viewport and HUD behavior.

nodes.description: Add artifact follow-up viewport bridge support. Update project/contracts/Contracts.Core/IViewportBridge.cs and project/hosts/complete-app/Scripts/GodotViewportBridge.cs so the viewport bridge can publish artifact follow-up suggestion and submission events to Godot, including concrete event record types queued through the existing bridge. Edit only those two files. Do not change actor logic or HUD code here.

Update only project/plugins/Plugin.Actors/ViewportActor.cs so it forwards artifact follow-up suggestion and submission events into the viewport bridge. Assume followup-viewport-contracts has already added the bridge methods. Keep existing viewport behavior unchanged for all other events.

Create or update only project/tests/Plugin.Actors.Tests/ViewportActorTests.cs to prove ViewportActor forwards artifact follow-up suggested and submitted events through the viewport bridge. Do not edit production code. Assume the viewport contract and routing tasks have already completed.

Update only project/hosts/complete-app/Scripts/HudController.cs so artifact follow-up suggestion and submission viewport events become visible in the HUD console in a concise, readable form. Do not modify actor logic, bridge code, or other host files.

Review the artifact follow-up visibility feature across the viewport contract, viewport actor routing, HUD console presentation, and tests. Tighten any small inconsistencies needed for the batch to build and verify cleanly without expanding scope.
